{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11521943]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sugiyama Shrine |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 07.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|Kohoku Ward]]{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 |Location Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Small Shikinaisha, former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]] |Sacred Event = |Map = {{location map|Yokohama|label={{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|Kohoku Ward]]. It is the local shrine of former {{ill|Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|ja|新田村 (神奈川県)|lt=Nittamura|lt_ja=新田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|1=Nitta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503739}} Yoshida and is regarded as one of the prominent {{ill|Ronsya|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ronsya|lt_ja=論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsya|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} of “{{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}”, the only former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Yokohama City and in old [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} (Isotakeru) * {{ill|Associated Deities|ja|deity#主神・配神|lt=Associated Deities|lt_ja=配祀神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Associated Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|Gongorō Kagemasa]], {{ill|Hino Seibyō|ja|比野聖廟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Seibyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 武蔵の五十猛命 神奈川県横浜市：新吉田町　杉山神社]（神奈備） *: ※[[:en:1908|1908]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]41st year ) it enshrined three deities within Nittamura[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁）. == History and Origins == A theory holds that a family which migrated from [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]] enshrined its ancestors here. Although the founding period is unknown, the ''{{ill|Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja|武蔵式社記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' states, “In Yoshida of Tsuzuki District, there is a small place called Sugiyama where the shrine is located,” while the manuscript compiled during the {{ill|Bunka period|ja|Bunka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}・[[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei period]] (1804–1829) – ''{{ill|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'' – records details such as “An expanse of two dan and five u, north of the village, with a flight of several stone steps leading up to a torii [[:en:Torii|Torii]] erected before the shrine: the main hall spans two ken by two and a half ken, and the haiden, facing south, spans three ken by two ken, with the sacred object immovable, belonging to the village’s Shofuku-ji {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}},” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}. It is said that the shrine’s sacred treasures and historical records, which were once kept at Shofuku-ji, the temple acting as its Bettō-ji, were completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html 武蔵国都筑郡唯一の式内社杉山神社を推理]（津軽古代王国）. In [[:en:1908|1908]], the shrine was Ranked among the local shrines in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District (which became Nittamura after the 1889 merger). == As a Ronsya of Shikinaisha == The theory that this shrine is a Shikinaisha existed even before the compilation of the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript''. In works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' (first published in 1966 and revised in 1972 as ''Musashi no Koshā'') by {{ill|Saitō Yoshihiko|ja|斎藤義彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Yoshihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Shintoist of the Bakumatsu period, and local historian {{ill|Hishinuma Isamu|ja|菱沼勇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishinuma Isamu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[http://www6.plala.or.jp/guti/cemetery/PERSON/H/hishinuma_i.html Hishinuma Isamu]（歴史が眠る多磨霊園）, this view is considered persuasive.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): No.52 Sugiyama Shrine – a Prominent Candidate]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所） Hishinuma Isamu bases his argument on several points: the existence of a minor place name “Sugiyama” in the area {{ill|Ko-aza|ja|小字|lt=small name|lt_ja=小名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ko-aza|1=koaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5327509}}, the fact that the Mori family – an old family in the region closely connected with the shrine – is said to have a history spanning over a thousand years according to oral tradition {{ill|oral tradition|ja|口伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oral tradition|1=oral tradition|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410744}}, and the presence of “Asama Tsumuka”, an ancient tomb ([[:en:Kofun|ancient tomb]]) on a hill (target: [[:en:Hill|elevated area]]) behind the Mori family that attests to the former power of a local warlord {{ill|local warlord|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local warlord|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, May 2003): No.53 Sugiyama Shrine in the Ward]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所）. In addition, the local ''History of Kohoku Ward'' mentions that the “Mori” in the Mori family is derived from the “guardian forest” ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Sacred Grove]]) and that the family is locally reverenced as “Sugiyama-sama”. Moreover, nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme there exist the [[{{ill|North River Upper Ruins|ja|北川表の上遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Upper Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] and [[{{ill|North River Shell Mound|ja|北川貝塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Shell Mound|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401676}}]], and the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village'' records that “a large shell mound existed in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}, with the local name “Shell Mound Field” still in use – evidence that archaeological sites in the area have long been known. Like many other ronsya, the shrine is situated on an elevated spot along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] of the [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari-sama]] * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神神社|lt=Tenjin-sama|lt_ja=天神様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443287}} == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Reitaisai|lt_ja=例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}：[[:en:October 9|October 9]] (Autumn Grand Festival) == Other Information == The shrine does not have a resident Shinto priest ([[:en:Kannushi|Shinto Priest]]) and is concurrently served by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 八幡社] located in [[:en:Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|Hodogaya Ward]], Setoketani Town. == Location and Access == [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|Kohoku Ward]]{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 * A 15–20 minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Yamata Station|Hokuyamada Station]] on the [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Torii at the front (south side) File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Torii on the northwest side File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|volume=Volume 84 of Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Home Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly around Yokohama City. Besides this shrine, there are the following prominent ronsya: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – One of the six great deities of Bushu, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Provincial Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida Town)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=An Investigation into the Only Shikinaisha in Tsuzuki District, Musashi Province: Sugiyama Shrine|date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) – A study proposing that it was enshrined by the descendants of the Santei people. {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|杉山神社|version=102215819|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杉山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11521943}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It is the local shrine of former {{ill|Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|ja|新田村 (神奈川県)|lt=Nittamura|lt_ja=新田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|1=Nitta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503739}} Yoshida and is regarded as one of the prominent {{ill|Ronsya|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ronsya|lt_ja=論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsya|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} of “{{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}”, the only former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Yokohama City and in old [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} (Isotakeru) * {{ill|Associated Deities|ja|deity#主神・配神|lt=Associated Deities|lt_ja=配祀神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Associated Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|Gongorō Kagemasa]], {{ill|Hino Seibyō|ja|比野聖廟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Seibyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 武蔵の五十猛命 神奈川県横浜市：新吉田町　杉山神社]（神奈備） *: ※[[:en:1908|1908]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]41st year ) it enshrined three deities within Nittamura[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁）. == History and Origins == A theory holds that a family which migrated from [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]] enshrined its ancestors here. Although the founding period is unknown, the ''{{ill|Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja|武蔵式社記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' states, “In Yoshida of Tsuzuki District, there is a small place called Sugiyama where the shrine is located,” while the manuscript compiled during the {{ill|Bunka period|ja|Bunka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}・[[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei period]] (1804–1829) – ''{{ill|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'' – records details such as “An expanse of two dan and five u, north of the village, with a flight of several stone steps leading up to a torii [[:en:Torii|Torii]] erected before the shrine: the main hall spans two ken by two and a half ken, and the haiden, facing south, spans three ken by two ken, with the sacred object immovable, belonging to the village’s Shofuku-ji {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}},” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}. It is said that the shrine’s sacred treasures and historical records, which were once kept at Shofuku-ji, the temple acting as its Bettō-ji, were completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html 武蔵国都筑郡唯一の式内社杉山神社を推理]（津軽古代王国）. In [[:en:1908|1908]], the shrine was Ranked among the local shrines in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District (which became Nittamura after the 1889 merger). == As a Ronsya of Shikinaisha == The theory that this shrine is a Shikinaisha existed even before the compilation of the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript''. In works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' (first published in 1966 and revised in 1972 as ''Musashi no Koshā'') by {{ill|Saitō Yoshihiko|ja|斎藤義彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Yoshihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Shintoist of the Bakumatsu period, and local historian {{ill|Hishinuma Isamu|ja|菱沼勇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishinuma Isamu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[http://www6.plala.or.jp/guti/cemetery/PERSON/H/hishinuma_i.html Hishinuma Isamu]（歴史が眠る多磨霊園）, this view is considered persuasive.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): No.52 Sugiyama Shrine – a Prominent Candidate]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所） Hishinuma Isamu bases his argument on several points: the existence of a minor place name “Sugiyama” in the area {{ill|Ko-aza|ja|小字|lt=small name|lt_ja=小名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ko-aza|1=koaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5327509}}, the fact that the Mori family – an old family in the region closely connected with the shrine – is said to have a history spanning over a thousand years according to oral tradition {{ill|oral tradition|ja|口伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oral tradition|1=oral tradition|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410744}}, and the presence of “Asama Tsumuka”, an ancient tomb ([[:en:Kofun|ancient tomb]]) on a hill (target: [[:en:Hill|elevated area]]) behind the Mori family that attests to the former power of a local warlord {{ill|local warlord|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local warlord|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, May 2003): No.53 Sugiyama Shrine in the Ward]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所）. In addition, the local ''History of Kohoku Ward'' mentions that the “Mori” in the Mori family is derived from the “guardian forest” ([[:en:Chinju no Mori|Sacred Grove]]) and that the family is locally reverenced as “Sugiyama-sama”. Moreover, nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme there exist the [[{{ill|North River Upper Ruins|ja|北川表の上遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Upper Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] and [[{{ill|North River Shell Mound|ja|北川貝塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Shell Mound|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401676}}]], and the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village'' records that “a large shell mound existed in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}, with the local name “Shell Mound Field” still in use – evidence that archaeological sites in the area have long been known. Like many other ronsya, the shrine is situated on an elevated spot along the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]] of the [[:en:Drainage system (geomorphology)|water system]] ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari-sama]] * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神神社|lt=Tenjin-sama|lt_ja=天神様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443287}} == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Reitaisai|lt_ja=例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}：[[:en:October 9|October 9]] (Autumn Grand Festival) == Other Information == The shrine does not have a resident Shinto priest ([[:en:Kannushi|Shinto Priest]]) and is concurrently served by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 八幡社] located in [[:en:Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|Hodogaya Ward]], Setoketani Town. == Location and Access == [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|Kohoku Ward]]{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 * A 15–20 minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Yamata Station|Hokuyamada Station]] on the [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Torii at the front (south side) File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Torii on the northwest side File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|volume=Volume 84 of Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Home Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly around Yokohama City. Besides this shrine, there are the following prominent ronsya: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – One of the six great deities of Bushu, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Provincial Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida Town)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=An Investigation into the Only Shikinaisha in Tsuzuki District, Musashi Province: Sugiyama Shrine|date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) – A study proposing that it was enshrined by the descendants of the Santei people. {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|杉山神社|version=102215819|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杉山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11521943}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11521943]] Wikidata name: Sugiyama shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sugiyama Shrine |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 07.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|Kohoku Ward]]{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 |Location Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = Small Shikinaisha, former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]] |Sacred Event = |Map = {{location map|Yokohama|label={{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|Kohoku Ward]]. It is the local shrine of former {{ill|Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|ja|新田村 (神奈川県)|lt=Nittamura|lt_ja=新田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|1=Nitta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503739}} Yoshida and is regarded as one of the prominent {{ill|Ronsya|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ronsya|lt_ja=論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsya|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} of “{{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}”, the only former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Yokohama City and in old [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]. == Deity == * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} (Isotakeru) * {{ill|Associated Deities|ja|deity#主神・配神|lt=Associated Deities|lt_ja=配祀神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Associated Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|Gongorō Kagemasa]], {{ill|Hino Seibyō|ja|比野聖廟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Seibyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 武蔵の五十猛命 神奈川県横浜市：新吉田町　杉山神社]（神奈備） *: ※[[:en:1908|1908]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]41st year ) it enshrined three deities within Nittamura[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁）. == History and Origins == A theory holds that a family which migrated from [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō Peninsula]] enshrined its ancestors here. Although the founding period is unknown, the ''{{ill|Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja|武蔵式社記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' states, “In Yoshida of Tsuzuki District, there is a small place called Sugiyama where the shrine is located,” while the manuscript compiled during the {{ill|Bunka period|ja|Bunka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}・[[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei period]] (1804–1829) – ''{{ill|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'' – records details such as “An expanse of two dan and five u, north of the village, with a flight of several stone steps leading up to a torii [[:en:Torii|Torii]] erected before the shrine: the main hall spans two ken by two and a half ken, and the haiden, facing south, spans three ken by two ken, with the sacred object immovable, belonging to the village’s Shofuku-ji {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}},” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}. In [[:en:1908|1908]], the shrine was Ranked among the local shrines in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District (which became Nittamura after the 1889 merger). == As a Ronsya of Shikinaisha == The theory that this shrine is a Shikinaisha existed even before the compilation of the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript''. In works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' (first published in 1966 and revised in 1972 as ''Musashi no Koshā'') by {{ill|Saitō Yoshihiko|ja|斎藤義彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Yoshihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Shintoist of the Bakumatsu period, and local historian {{ill|Hishinuma Isamu|ja|菱沼勇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishinuma Isamu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[http://www6.plala.or.jp/guti/cemetery/PERSON/H/hishinuma_i.html Hishinuma Isamu]（歴史が眠る多磨霊園）, this view is considered persuasive.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): No.52 Sugiyama Shrine – a Prominent Candidate]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所） Hishinuma Isamu bases his argument on several points: the existence of a minor place name “Sugiyama” in the area {{ill|Ko-aza|ja|小字|lt=small name|lt_ja=小名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ko-aza|1=koaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5327509}}, the fact that the Mori family – an old family in the region closely connected with the shrine – is said to have a history spanning over a thousand years according to oral tradition {{ill|oral tradition|ja|口伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oral tradition|1=oral tradition|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410744}}, and the presence of “Asama Tsumuka”, an ancient tomb ([[:en:Kofun|ancient tomb]]) on a hill (target: [[:en:Hill|elevated area]]) behind the Mori family that attests to the former power of a local warlord {{ill|local warlord|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local warlord|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, May 2003): No.53 Sugiyama Shrine in the Ward]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所）. River|ja|早渕川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari-sama]] * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神神社|lt=Tenjin-sama|lt_ja=天神様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443287}} == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Reitaisai|lt_ja=例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}：[[:en:October 9|October 9]] (Autumn Grand Festival) == Other Information == The shrine does not have a resident Shinto priest ([[:en:Kannushi|Shinto Priest]]) and is concurrently served by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 八幡社] located in [[:en:Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|Hodogaya Ward]], Setoketani Town. == Location and Access == [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|Kohoku Ward]]{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 * A 15–20 minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Yamata Station|Hokuyamada Station]] on the [[:en:Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line]] == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Torii at the front (south side) File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Torii on the northwest side File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|volume=Volume 84 of Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Home Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly around Yokohama City. Besides this shrine, there are the following prominent ronsya: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – One of the six great deities of Bushu, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Provincial Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida Town)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=An Investigation into the Only Shikinaisha in Tsuzuki District, Musashi Province: Sugiyama Shrine|date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) – A study proposing that it was enshrined by the descendants of the Santei people. {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|杉山神社|version=102215819|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杉山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11521943}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sugiyama Shrine |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 07.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}}{{ill|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1358965|lt=Kohoku Ward}}{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 |Location Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = Small Shikinaisha, former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |festival = {{ill|October 9|en|October 9|WD=Q2923|lt=October 9}} |Sacred Event = |Map = {{location map|Yokohama|label={{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}}, {{ill|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1358965|lt=Kohoku Ward}}. It is the local shrine of former {{ill|Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|ja|新田村 (神奈川県)|lt=Nittamura|lt_ja=新田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|1=Nitta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503739}} Yoshida and is regarded as one of the prominent {{ill|Ronsya|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ronsya|lt_ja=論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsya|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} of “{{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}”, the only former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Yokohama City and in old {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}. == Deity == * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} (Isotakeru) * {{ill|Associated Deities|ja|deity#主神・配神|lt=Associated Deities|lt_ja=配祀神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Associated Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: {{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|Hino Seibyō|ja|比野聖廟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Seibyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 武蔵の五十猛命 神奈川県横浜市：新吉田町　杉山神社]（神奈備） *: ※{{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}41st year ) it enshrined three deities within Nittamura[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁）. == History and Origins == A theory holds that a family which migrated from {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula}} enshrined its ancestors here. Although the founding period is unknown, the ''{{ill|Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja|武蔵式社記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' states, “In Yoshida of Tsuzuki District, there is a small place called Sugiyama where the shrine is located,” while the manuscript compiled during the {{ill|Bunka period|ja|Bunka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}・{{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei period}} (1804–1829) – ''{{ill|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'' – records details such as “An expanse of two dan and five u, north of the village, with a flight of several stone steps leading up to a torii {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} erected before the shrine: the main hall spans two ken by two and a half ken, and the haiden, facing south, spans three ken by two ken, with the sacred object immovable, belonging to the village’s Shofuku-ji {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}},” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}. It is said that the shrine’s sacred treasures and historical records, which were once kept at Shofuku-ji, the temple acting as its Bettō-ji, were completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html 武蔵国都筑郡唯一の式内社杉山神社を推理]（津軽古代王国）. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}}, the shrine was Ranked among the local shrines in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District (which became Nittamura after the 1889 merger). == As a Ronsya of Shikinaisha == The theory that this shrine is a Shikinaisha existed even before the compilation of the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript''. In works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' (first published in 1966 and revised in 1972 as ''Musashi no Koshā'') by {{ill|Saitō Yoshihiko|ja|斎藤義彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Yoshihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Shintoist of the Bakumatsu period, and local historian {{ill|Hishinuma Isamu|ja|菱沼勇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishinuma Isamu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[http://www6.plala.or.jp/guti/cemetery/PERSON/H/hishinuma_i.html Hishinuma Isamu]（歴史が眠る多磨霊園）, this view is considered persuasive.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): No.52 Sugiyama Shrine – a Prominent Candidate]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所） Hishinuma Isamu bases his argument on several points: the existence of a minor place name “Sugiyama” in the area {{ill|Ko-aza|ja|小字|lt=small name|lt_ja=小名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ko-aza|1=koaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5327509}}, the fact that the Mori family – an old family in the region closely connected with the shrine – is said to have a history spanning over a thousand years according to oral tradition {{ill|oral tradition|ja|口伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oral tradition|1=oral tradition|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410744}}, and the presence of “Asama Tsumuka”, an ancient tomb ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=ancient tomb}}) on a hill (target: {{ill|Hill|en|Hill|WD=Q54050|lt=elevated area}}) behind the Mori family that attests to the former power of a local warlord {{ill|local warlord|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local warlord|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, May 2003): No.53 Sugiyama Shrine in the Ward]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所）. In addition, the local ''History of Kohoku Ward'' mentions that the “Mori” in the Mori family is derived from the “guardian forest” ({{ill|Chinju no Mori|en|Chinju no Mori|WD=Q11651158|lt=Sacred Grove}}) and that the family is locally reverenced as “Sugiyama-sama”. Moreover, nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme there exist the [[{{ill|North River Upper Ruins|ja|北川表の上遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Upper Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] and [[{{ill|North River Shell Mound|ja|北川貝塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Shell Mound|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401676}}]], and the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village'' records that “a large shell mound existed in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}, with the local name “Shell Mound Field” still in use – evidence that archaeological sites in the area have long been known. Like many other ronsya, the shrine is situated on an elevated spot along the {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|WD=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River}} of the {{ill|Drainage system (geomorphology)|en|Drainage system (geomorphology)|WD=Q285451|lt=water system}} ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神神社|lt=Tenjin-sama|lt_ja=天神様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443287}} == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Reitaisai|lt_ja=例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}：{{ill|October 9|en|October 9|WD=Q2923|lt=October 9}} (Autumn Grand Festival) == Other Information == The shrine does not have a resident Shinto priest ([[:en:Kannushi|Shinto Priest]]) and is concurrently served by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 八幡社] located in {{ill|Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|en|Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1192964|lt=Hodogaya Ward}}, Setoketani Town. == Location and Access == {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}}{{ill|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1358965|lt=Kohoku Ward}}{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 * A 15–20 minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Yamata Station|en|Higashi-Yamata Station|WD=Q11103294|lt=Hokuyamada Station}} on the {{ill|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|en|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|WD=Q910329|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line}} == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Torii at the front (south side) File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Torii on the northwest side File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|volume=Volume 84 of Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Home Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly around Yokohama City. Besides this shrine, there are the following prominent ronsya: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – One of the six great deities of Bushu, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Provincial Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida Town)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=An Investigation into the Only Shikinaisha in Tsuzuki District, Musashi Province: Sugiyama Shrine|date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) – A study proposing that it was enshrined by the descendants of the Santei people. {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|杉山神社|version=102215819|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杉山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11521943}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sugiyama Shrine |image = [[File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 07.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}}{{ill|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1358965|lt=Kohoku Ward}}{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 |Location Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = Small Shikinaisha, former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |festival = {{ill|October 9|en|October 9|WD=Q2923|lt=October 9}} |Sacred Event = |Map = {{location map|Yokohama|label={{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (杉山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}}, {{ill|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1358965|lt=Kohoku Ward}}. It is the local shrine of former {{ill|Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|ja|新田村 (神奈川県)|lt=Nittamura|lt_ja=新田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nittamura (Kanagawa Prefecture)|1=Nitta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503739}} Yoshida and is regarded as one of the prominent {{ill|Ronsya|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ronsya|lt_ja=論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsya|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} of “{{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}”, the only former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Yokohama City and in old {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}. == Deity == * {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}} (Isotakeru) * {{ill|Associated Deities|ja|deity#主神・配神|lt=Associated Deities|lt_ja=配祀神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Associated Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: {{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|Hino Seibyō|ja|比野聖廟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Seibyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 武蔵の五十猛命 神奈川県横浜市：新吉田町　杉山神社]（神奈備） *: ※{{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}41st year ) it enshrined three deities within Nittamura[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁）. == History and Origins == A theory holds that a family which migrated from {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula}} enshrined its ancestors here. Although the founding period is unknown, the ''{{ill|Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja|武蔵式社記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' states, “In Yoshida of Tsuzuki District, there is a small place called Sugiyama where the shrine is located,” while the manuscript compiled during the {{ill|Bunka period|ja|Bunka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}・{{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei period}} (1804–1829) – ''{{ill|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'' – records details such as “An expanse of two dan and five u, north of the village, with a flight of several stone steps leading up to a torii {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} erected before the shrine: the main hall spans two ken by two and a half ken, and the haiden, facing south, spans three ken by two ken, with the sacred object immovable, belonging to the village’s Shofuku-ji {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}},” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}. It is said that the shrine’s sacred treasures and historical records, which were once kept at Shofuku-ji, the temple acting as its Bettō-ji, were completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html 武蔵国都筑郡唯一の式内社杉山神社を推理]（津軽古代王国）. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}}, the shrine was Ranked among the local shrines in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District (which became Nittamura after the 1889 merger). == As a Ronsya of Shikinaisha == The theory that this shrine is a Shikinaisha existed even before the compilation of the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript''. In works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' (first published in 1966 and revised in 1972 as ''Musashi no Koshā'') by {{ill|Saitō Yoshihiko|ja|斎藤義彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Yoshihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Shintoist of the Bakumatsu period, and local historian {{ill|Hishinuma Isamu|ja|菱沼勇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishinuma Isamu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[http://www6.plala.or.jp/guti/cemetery/PERSON/H/hishinuma_i.html Hishinuma Isamu]（歴史が眠る多磨霊園）, this view is considered persuasive.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): No.52 Sugiyama Shrine – a Prominent Candidate]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所） Hishinuma Isamu bases his argument on several points: the existence of a minor place name “Sugiyama” in the area {{ill|Ko-aza|ja|小字|lt=small name|lt_ja=小名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ko-aza|1=koaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5327509}}, the fact that the Mori family – an old family in the region closely connected with the shrine – is said to have a history spanning over a thousand years according to oral tradition {{ill|oral tradition|ja|口伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oral tradition|1=oral tradition|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410744}}, and the presence of “Asama Tsumuka”, an ancient tomb ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=ancient tomb}}) on a hill (target: {{ill|Hill|en|Hill|WD=Q54050|lt=elevated area}}) behind the Mori family that attests to the former power of a local warlord {{ill|local warlord|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local warlord|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, May 2003): No.53 Sugiyama Shrine in the Ward]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所）. In addition, the local ''History of Kohoku Ward'' mentions that the “Mori” in the Mori family is derived from the “guardian forest” ({{ill|Chinju no Mori|en|Chinju no Mori|WD=Q11651158|lt=Sacred Grove}}) and that the family is locally reverenced as “Sugiyama-sama”. Moreover, nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme there exist the [[{{ill|North River Upper Ruins|ja|北川表の上遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Upper Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] and [[{{ill|North River Shell Mound|ja|北川貝塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Shell Mound|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401676}}]], and the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village'' records that “a large shell mound existed in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}, with the local name “Shell Mound Field” still in use – evidence that archaeological sites in the area have long been known. Like many other ronsya, the shrine is situated on an elevated spot along the {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|WD=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River}} of the {{ill|Drainage system (geomorphology)|en|Drainage system (geomorphology)|WD=Q285451|lt=water system}} ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神神社|lt=Tenjin-sama|lt_ja=天神様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443287}} == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Reitaisai|lt_ja=例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}：{{ill|October 9|en|October 9|WD=Q2923|lt=October 9}} (Autumn Grand Festival) == Other Information == The shrine does not have a resident Shinto priest ([[:en:Kannushi|Shinto Priest]]) and is concurrently served by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 八幡社] located in {{ill|Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|en|Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1192964|lt=Hodogaya Ward}}, Setoketani Town. == Location and Access == {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}}{{ill|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1358965|lt=Kohoku Ward}}{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 * A 15–20 minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Yamata Station|en|Higashi-Yamata Station|WD=Q11103294|lt=Hokuyamada Station}} on the {{ill|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|en|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|WD=Q910329|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line}} == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Torii at the front (south side) File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Torii on the northwest side File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|volume=Volume 84 of Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Home Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly around Yokohama City. Besides this shrine, there are the following prominent ronsya: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – One of the six great deities of Bushu, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Provincial Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida Town)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=An Investigation into the Only Shikinaisha in Tsuzuki District, Musashi Province: Sugiyama Shrine|date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) – A study proposing that it was enshrined by the descendants of the Santei people. {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|杉山神社|version=102215819|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杉山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11521943}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:pages]]

based on wikidata|WD=Q11503739}} Yoshida and is regarded as one of the prominent {{ill|Ronsya|ja|延喜式神名帳#論社|lt=Ronsya|lt_ja=論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsya|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} of “{{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}}”, the only former enshrined three deities within Nittamura[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 杉山神社]（神奈川県神社庁）. == History and Origins == A theory holds that a family which migrated from {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula}} enshrined its ancestors here. Although the founding period is unknown, the ''{{ill|Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja|武蔵式社記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Shikishiki-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' states, “In Yoshida of Tsuzuki District, there is a small place called Sugiyama where the shrine is located,” while the manuscript compiled during the {{ill|Bunka period|ja|Bunka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}・{{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei period}} (1804–1829) – ''{{ill|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'' – records details such as “An expanse of two dan and five u, north of the village, with a flight of several stone steps leading up to a torii {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} erected before the shrine: the main hall spans two ken by two and a half ken, and the haiden, facing south, spans three ken by two ken, with the sacred object immovable, belonging to the village’s Shofuku-ji {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}},” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}. It is said that the shrine’s sacred treasures and historical records, which were once kept at Shofuku-ji, the temple acting as its Bettō-ji, were completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html 武蔵国都筑郡唯一の式内社杉山神社を推理]（津軽古代王国）. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}}, the shrine was Ranked among the local shrines in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District (which became Nittamura after the 1889 merger). == As a Ronsya of Shikinaisha == The theory that this shrine is a Shikinaisha existed even before the compilation of the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript''. In works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' (first published in 1966 and revised in 1972 as ''Musashi no Koshā'') by {{ill|Saitō Yoshihiko|ja|斎藤義彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitō Yoshihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a Shintoist of the Bakumatsu period, and local historian {{ill|Hishinuma Isamu|ja|菱沼勇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishinuma Isamu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[http://www6.plala.or.jp/guti/cemetery/PERSON/H/hishinuma_i.html Hishinuma Isamu]（歴史が眠る多磨霊園）, this view is considered persuasive.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, April 2003): No.52 Sugiyama Shrine – a Prominent Candidate]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所） Hishinuma Isamu bases his argument on several points: the existence of a minor place name “Sugiyama” in the area {{ill|Ko-aza|ja|小字|lt=small name|lt_ja=小名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ko-aza|1=koaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5327509}}, the fact that the Mori family – an old family in the region closely connected with the shrine – is said to have a history spanning over a thousand years according to oral tradition {{ill|oral tradition|ja|口伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oral tradition|1=oral tradition|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410744}}, and the presence of “Asama Tsumuka”, an ancient tomb ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=ancient tomb}}) on a hill (target: {{ill|Hill|en|Hill|WD=Q54050|lt=elevated area}}) behind the Mori family that attests to the former power of a local warlord {{ill|local warlord|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local warlord|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}}.[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kohoku Ward (Series: Our Town Kohoku, May 2003): No.53 Sugiyama Shrine in the Ward]（公益財団法人 大倉精神文化研究所）. In addition, the local ''History of Kohoku Ward'' mentions that the “Mori” in the Mori family is derived from the “guardian forest” ({{ill|Chinju no Mori|en|Chinju no Mori|WD=Q11651158|lt=Sacred Grove}}) and that the family is locally reverenced as “Sugiyama-sama”. Moreover, nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme there exist the [[{{ill|North River Upper Ruins|ja|北川表の上遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Upper Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] and [[{{ill|North River Shell Mound|ja|北川貝塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North River Shell Mound|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401676}}]], and the ''Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village'' records that “a large shell mound existed in Yoshida Village of Tsuzuki District” {{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}, with the local name “Shell Mound Field” still in use – evidence that archaeological sites in the area have long been known. Like many other ronsya, the shrine is situated on an elevated spot along the {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|WD=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River}} of the {{ill|Drainage system (geomorphology)|en|Drainage system (geomorphology)|WD=Q285451|lt=water system}} ({{ill|Hayabuchi River|ja|早渕川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabuchi River|1=Hayabuchi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511423}}). == Shrines within the Precincts == * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama}} * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神神社|lt=Tenjin-sama|lt_ja=天神様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjin Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443287}} == Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Reitaisai|lt_ja=例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}：{{ill|October 9|en|October 9|WD=Q2923|lt=October 9}} (Autumn Grand Festival) == Other Information == The shrine does not have a resident Shinto priest ([[:en:Kannushi|Shinto Priest]]) and is concurrently served by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 八幡社] located in {{ill|Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|en|Hodogaya-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1192964|lt=Hodogaya Ward}}, Setoketani Town. == Location and Access == {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}}{{ill|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1358965|lt=Kohoku Ward}}{{ill|Shinyoshida Town|ja|新吉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyoshida Town|1=Shin-yoshida-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97180184}}4509 * A 15–20 minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Yamata Station|en|Higashi-Yamata Station|WD=Q11103294|lt=Hokuyamada Station}} on the {{ill|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|en|Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|WD=Q910329|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line}} == Gallery == File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Torii at the front (south side) File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Torii on the northwest side File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama within the precincts == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|volume=Volume 84 of Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Home Ministry Geographical Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki Manuscript, Yoshida Village}}}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – Dozens of shrines are distributed mainly around Yokohama City. Besides this shrine, there are the following prominent ronsya: ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市緑区西八朔町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} – One of the six great deities of Bushu, also participating in the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi Provincial Festival). ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区茅ケ崎中央)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Sugiyama Shrine|ja|杉山神社 (横浜市都筑区中川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社 (横浜市)|1=Tsurumi Shrine|commons=Tsurumi-jinja, Yokohama|2=ja|3=鶴見神社 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676163}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shinyoshida Town)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=An Investigation into the Only Shikinaisha in Tsuzuki District, Musashi Province: Sugiyama Shrine|date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) – A study proposing that it was enshrined by the descendants of the Santei people. {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|杉山神社|version=102215819|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杉山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11521943}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:pages]]
